steps.title: Understand what we're measuring
steps.content: Standard deviation measures how spread out data is from the center. The smallest standard deviation belongs to the dataset where values are closest together.

steps.title: Compare tightness of each dataset
steps.content:
Look at how tightly values cluster:
  • A: 44, 45, 47, 49, 50 — spreads from 44 to 50 (range = 6)
  • B: 45, 45, 47, 49, 49 — spreads from 45 to 49 (range = 4), with pairs repeated
  • C: 45, 46, 47, 48, 49 — spreads from 45 to 49 (range = 4), evenly spaced
  • D: 46, 47, 47, 47, 48 — spreads from 46 to 48 (range = 2), with three values identical at 47
Choice D has both the smallest range and the most dramatic clustering. That matches choice D — D is correct.
